popularStory.headline: Rev John Lathrop
popularStory.description: John Lothropp (also Lothrop or Lathrop; 1584–1653) was an English Anglican clergyman, who became a Congregationalist minister and emigrant to New England. He was the founder of Barnstable, Massachuset …

zh.lifeSummary: 當Hannah Howse於1594年5月5日在英國肯特艾格頓受洗時，她的父親Rev. John Howse27歲，母親Alice Lloyd21歲。她於1610年與John Lothrop在英國英格蘭肯特易亞斯特維爾結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和3個女兒的父母。她去世於大約1633年在英國米德萊可倫敦享年40歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Hannah Howse于1594年5月5日在英国肯特艾格頓受洗时，她的父亲Rev. John Howse27岁，母亲Alice Lloyd21岁。她于1610年与John Lothrop在英国英格兰肯特易亞斯特維爾结婚。他们至少是5个儿子和3个女儿的父母。她去世于大约1633年在英国米德萊可倫敦享年40岁。

nameMeanings.name: Howse
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.

variants.name: House
meanings: English: topographic or occupational name for someone employed ‘at the house’, from Middle English hous(e) (Old English hūs), probably a religious house such as a convent. In the Middle Ages the majority of the population lived in cottages or huts rather than houses.

English: perhaps a topographic name from an unrecorded Middle English huse, a southwest dialect form of Old English hyse ‘place overgrown with (water) plants’, a derivative of Old English hos ‘(water) plant’. In modern English, the name might have been absorbed into Huss or Hose .

nameMeanings.name: Hannah
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Biblical name, borne by the mother of the prophet Samuel (1 Samuel 1:2), Hebrew Hanna. It is derived from a Hebrew word meaning ‘He (i.e. God) has favoured me (i.e. with a child)’. See also Anne . This form of the name was taken up as a given name by the Puritans in the 16th and 17th centuries and remained popular until the late 19th century. Thereafter it fell somewhat from favour but has enjoyed a massive revival since the 1990s.

variants.name: Hanna
meanings: Either the biblical name ( see Hannah ) or a short form of Johanna .

variants.name: Hana
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

en.lifeSummary: When Hannah Howse was christened on 5 May 1594, in Egerton, Kent, England, her father, Rev. John Howse, was 27 and her mother, Alice Lloyd, was 21. She married John Lothrop in 1610, in Eastwell, Kent, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 3 daughters. She died about 1633, in London, Middlesex, England, at the age of 40.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Hannah Howse a été baptisée le 5 mai 1594, à Egerton, Kent, Angleterre, son père, Rev. John Howse, avait 27 ans et sa mère, Alice Lloyd, avait 21 ans. Elle a épousé John Lothrop en 1610, à Eastwell, Kent, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils et 3 filles. Elle est décédée environ 1633, à Londres, Middlesex, Angleterre, à l’âge de 40 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Hannah Howse am 5. Mai 1594 in Egerton, Kent, England getauft wurde, war ihr Vater Rev. John Howse 27 und ihre Mutter Alice Lloyd 21. Sie hat 1610 in Eastwell, Kent, England, United Kingdom John Lothrop geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 5 Söhne und 3 Töchter. Sie starb um 1633 im Alter von 40 Jahren in London, Middlesex, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Hannah Howse è stato battezzato il 5 maggio 1594 nel Egerton, Kent, England, sua padre, Rev. John Howse, aveva 27 anni e sua madre, Alice Lloyd, aveva 21 anni. Lei sposato John Lothrop nel 1610, nel Eastwell, Kent, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 5 figli e 3 figlie. Morì circa 1633, nel London, Middlesex, England, all'età di 40 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Hannah Howseが1594年5月5日にイギリスケントエジャートンで洗礼を受けた時，父Rev. John Howseは27歳，母Alice Lloydは21歳であった。1610年にイギリスイギリスケントエアストウエルでJohn Lothropと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも5人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼女は約1633年にイギリスミッドレセックスロンドンで40歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Hannah Howse(이)가 1594년 5월 5일 영국 켄트 거턴에서 침례를 받았을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Rev. John Howse(은)는 27세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Alice Lloyd(은)는 21세였다. 그녀는 1610년 영국 잉글랜드 켄트 이스트웰에서 John Lothrop(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 대략 1633년 영국 미들레세스 런던에서 40세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Hannah Howse foi batizada em 5 de maio de 1594, em Egerton, Kent, Inglaterra, seu pai, Rev. John Howse, tinha 27 anos e sua mãe, Alice Lloyd, tinha 21 anos. Ela casou-se com John Lothrop em 1610, em Eastwell, Kent, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 5 filhos e 3 filhas. Ela faleceu aproximadamente 1633, em London, Middlesex, Inglaterra, com 40 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Hannah Howse была крещена 5 мая 1594 г. в местности эгиртон, кэнт, Англия, её отцу по имени Rev. John Howse было 27 лет, а матери, которую звали Alice Lloyd, 21. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени John Lothrop в 1610 г. в местности эаствелл, кэнт, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 3 дочери. Она умерла около 1633 г. в местности Лондон, миддлесекс, Англия в возрасте 40 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Hannah Howse se bautizó el 5 de mayo de 1594, en Egerton, Kent, Inglaterra, su papá, Rev. John Howse, tenía 27 años y su mamá, Alice Lloyd, tenía 21 años. Se casó con John Lothrop en 1610, en Eastwell, Kent, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 5 hijos y 3 hijas. Murió aproximadamente 1633, en London, Middlesex, Inglaterra, a la edad de 40 años.
